info.title: Primitive API
info.description:
The Primitive API lets you manage domains, emails, webhook endpoints,
filters, and account settings programmatically.

## Authentication

All endpoints require a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer prim_<your_api_key>
```

API keys are org-scoped. Create and manage them in your dashboard
under Settings > API Keys.

## Rate Limiting

The API enforces a sliding window rate limit of **120 requests per
60 seconds** per organization. When exceeded, the API returns `429`
with a `Retry-After` header indicating how many seconds to wait.

## Pagination

List endpoints use cursor-based pagination. Responses include a
`meta` object with `total`, `limit`, and `cursor` fields. Pass the
`cursor` value as a query parameter to fetch the next page. When
`cursor` is `null`, there are no more results.

## Response Format

All responses use a consistent envelope:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": { ... },
  "meta": { "total": 42, "limit": 50, "cursor": "..." }
}
```

Errors follow the same pattern:

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": { "code": "not_found", "message": "Email not found" }
}
```

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Manage your account settings, storage, and webhook secret

tags.name: Domains
tags.description: Claim, verify, and manage email domains

tags.name: Emails
tags.description: List, inspect, and manage received emails

tags.name: Sending
tags.description: Send outbound emails through the Primitive API

tags.name: Endpoints
tags.description: Manage webhook endpoints that receive email events

tags.name: Filters
tags.description: Manage whitelist and blocklist filter rules

tags.name: Webhook Deliveries
tags.description: View and replay webhook delivery attempts

get.description:
Returns the webhook signing secret for your account. If no secret
exists yet, one is generated automatically on first access.

post.description:
Generates a new webhook signing secret, replacing the current one.
Rate limited to once per 60 minutes.

get.description:
Returns all verified and unverified domains for your organization,
sorted by creation date (newest first). Each domain includes a
`verified` boolean to distinguish between the two states.

post.description:
Creates an unverified domain claim. You will receive a
`verification_token` to add as a DNS TXT record before
calling the verify endpoint.

409.description:
Domain claim conflicts with existing state. Two error codes
are possible:
  * `mx_conflict`: the domain's current MX records point at
    another mailbox provider. The response includes
    `error.details.mx_conflict` with the detected provider
    and a suggested subdomain.
  * `conflict`: the domain is already claimed by another
    org, or a pending claim exists for another user.

mx_conflict.summary: Domain has MX records on another provider

error.message: Domain is currently receiving mail via another provider

already_claimed.summary: Domain already claimed by another org

error.message: Domain is already claimed by another organization

patch.description:
Update a verified domain's settings. Only verified domains can be
updated. Per-domain spam thresholds require a Pro plan.

delete.description: Deletes a verified or unverified domain claim.

post.description:
Checks DNS records (MX and TXT) to verify domain ownership.
On success, the domain is promoted from unverified to verified.
On failure, returns which checks passed and which failed.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of received emails. Supports filtering by
domain, status, date range, and free-text search across subject,
sender, and recipient fields.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter emails created on or after this timestamp

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter emails created on or before this timestamp

get.description:
Downloads the raw RFC 822 email file (.eml). Authenticates via
a signed download token (provided in webhook payloads) or a
valid session.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Signed download token from webhook payload

X-Content-SHA256.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the file

get.description:
Downloads all attachments as a gzip-compressed tar archive.
Authenticates via a signed download token (provided in webhook
payloads) or a valid session.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Signed download token from webhook payload

X-Content-SHA256.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the archive

X-Attachment-Count.description: Number of attachments in the archive

post.description:
Re-delivers the webhook payload for this email to all active
endpoints matching the email's domain. Rate limited per-email
(short cooldown between successive replays of the same email)
and per-org (burst + sustained windows), sharing an org-wide
budget with delivery replays.

Creates a new webhook endpoint. If a deactivated endpoint with the
same URL and domain exists, it is reactivated instead.
Subject to plan limits on the number of active endpoints.

patch.description:
Updates an active webhook endpoint. If the URL is changed, the old
endpoint is deactivated and a new one is created (or an existing
deactivated endpoint with the new URL is reactivated).

delete.description:
Soft-deletes a webhook endpoint. The endpoint will no longer
receive webhook deliveries.

post.description:
Sends a sample `email.received` event to the endpoint. The request
includes SSRF protection (private IP rejection and DNS pinning).
Rate limited to 4 per minute and 30 per hour (non-exempt).
Successful deliveries and verified-domain endpoints are exempt
from the rate limit.

get.description: Returns all whitelist and blocklist filter rules.

post.description:
Creates a new whitelist or blocklist filter. Per-domain filters
require a Pro plan. Patterns are stored as lowercase.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of webhook delivery attempts. Each delivery
includes a nested `email` object with sender, recipient, and subject.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter deliveries created on or after this timestamp

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter deliveries created on or before this timestamp

post.description:
Re-sends the stored webhook payload from a previous delivery attempt.
If the original endpoint is still active, it is targeted. If the
original endpoint was deleted, the oldest active endpoint is used.
Deactivated endpoints cannot be replayed to. Rate limited per-org,
sharing an org-wide budget with email replays.

Sends a plain-text outbound email synchronously. The request stays
open until Primitive's downstream SMTP service completes the SMTP
transaction.

BearerAuth.description: API key with `prim_` prefix: `Authorization: Bearer prim_<key>`

DownloadToken.name: token
DownloadToken.description: Signed download token provided in webhook payloads

Cursor.name: cursor
Cursor.description:
Pagination cursor from a previous response's `meta.cursor` field.
Format: `{ISO-datetime}|{id}`

Forbidden.description: Authenticated caller lacks permission for the operation

PayloadTooLarge.description: Request body exceeds the supported size limit

error.message: Body must be 65536 bytes or smaller

BadGateway.description: Primitive could not complete the downstream SMTP request

GatewayTimeout.description: Primitive timed out waiting for the downstream SMTP request

limit.description: Page size used for this request

cursor.description: Cursor for the next page, or null if no more results

details.description:
Optional structured data that callers can inspect to recover
from the error. The fields present depend on `code`. Additional
keys may be added over time without a major-version bump.

provider_name.description: Human-readable name of the detected mailbox provider (e.g. "Google Workspace").

spam_threshold.description: Global spam score threshold (0-15). Emails scoring above this are rejected. Set to null to disable.

discard_content_on_webhook_confirmed.description: Whether to discard email content after the webhook endpoint confirms receipt.

used_kb.description: Total storage used in kilobytes (1 decimal)

used_mb.description: Total storage used in megabytes (2 decimals)

quota_mb.description: Storage quota in megabytes (based on plan)

Domain.description:
A domain can be either verified or unverified. Verified domains have
`is_active` and `spam_threshold` fields. Unverified domains have a
`verification_token` for DNS verification.

verification_token.description: Add this value as a TXT record to verify ownership

is_active.description: Whether the domain accepts incoming emails

spam_threshold.description: Per-domain spam threshold override (Pro plan required)

mxFound.description: Whether MX records point to Primitive

txtFound.description: Whether the TXT verification record was found

from_email.description: Parsed from address (from_header or sender fallback)

from.description: Active sender address on a domain owned by your organization

to.description: Exact recipient address that previously sent your org an authenticated inbound email

subject.description: Subject line for the outbound message

body.description: Plain-text message body. Maximum size is 65536 UTF-8 bytes.

smtp_code.description: Final SMTP status code reported by the downstream SMTP transaction

smtp_message.description: Final SMTP status message, if available

remote_host.description: Recipient MX host contacted for the SMTP transaction

service_message_id.description: Message identifier assigned by Primitive's outbound SMTP service

domain_id.description: Restrict this endpoint to emails from a specific domain

url.description: The webhook URL to deliver events to

domain_id.description: Restrict to emails from a specific domain

status.description: HTTP status code returned by the endpoint

signature.description: The signature header value sent (if webhook secret is configured)

domain_id.description: If set, filter applies only to this domain

pattern.description: Email address or pattern to match (stored lowercase)
Email address or pattern to filter

domain_id.description: Restrict filter to a specific domain (Pro plan required)
